title: How an exercise-activated enzyme helps to keep the brain young
fullText:
RESEARCH HIGHLIGHT
 25 February 2026
 
 

 

 
 
 
 A protein reinforces the blood–brain barrier, which becomes leaky with age.
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 An enzyme produced by liver cells (pictured) has anti-ageing effects on the brain. Credit: Biophoto Associates/Science Photo Library
 
 

 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 Exercise prevents brain ageing and memory loss by strengthening the blood–brain barrier, the structure that keeps harmful substances out of the brain1.
